data.title: Lowey Dannenberg Notifies Integral Ad Science Holding Corp. (“IAS” or the “Company”) (NASDAQ: IAS) Investors of Securities Class Action Lawsuit and Encourages Investors with more than $300,000 in Losses to Contact the Firm
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 12, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Lowey Dannenberg P.C., a preeminent law firm in obtaining redress for consumers and investors, announces the filing of a class action lawsuit against Integral Ad Science Holding Corp. (“IAS” or the “Company”) for violations of the federal securities laws on behalf of investors who purchased or acquired IAS common stock between March 2, 2023, and February 27, 2024, inclusive (the “Class Period”).

On January 29, 2025, a complaint was filed against the Company and certain of its current and former officers, alleging that throughout the Class Period, Defendants made false and/or misleading statements and/or failed to disclose that: (i) that IAS was experiencing a new material trend of increased competitive pricing pressures and that, as a result, IAS had been forced to cut prices to compensate for weakening demand and slowing revenue growth; (ii) that IAS’s pricing function was no longer “favorable” and IAS could not sustain its pricing and drive price increases; (iii) that pricing had become a key differentiator between IAS and its competitor necessary to close major renewals and new deals; (iv) that the risks that competition “could result in increased pricing pressure” or “could put pressure on us to change our prices” had in fact transpired; and (v) as a result, the IAS’s public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

When investors learned the truth, IAS’ common stock declined precipitously, injuring investors.

If you suffered a loss of more than $300,000 in IAS’ securities, and wish to participate, or learn more, click here, or please contact our attorneys at (914) 733-7256 or via email to Andrea Farah (afarah@lowey.com) or Vincent R. Cappucci Jr. (vcappucci@lowey.com).

Any investor who wishes to serve as Lead Plaintiff must act before March 31, 2025.

About Lowey Dannenberg

Lowey Dannenberg is a national firm representing institutional and individual investors, who suffered financial losses resulting from corporate fraud and malfeasance in violation of federal securities and antitrust laws. The firm has significant experience in prosecuting multi-million-dollar lawsuits and has recovered billions of dollars on behalf of its clients.

Contact:

Lowey Dannenberg P.C.

44 South Broadway, Suite 1100

White Plains, NY 10601

Tel: (914) 733-7234

Email: afarah@lowey.com

SOURCE: Lowey Dannenberg P.C.

data.title: Udemy Cut to Market Perform From Outperform by William Blair
U.S. Natural Gas Falls on Weather-Driven Demand Loss — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. natural gas futures are pulling back from more than two-year highs earlier in the week as participants start taking into account the loss of demand on warmer March weather forecasts. "The market is now finally coming back to realistic pricing," says long-time natural gas trader John Woods. An overreaction to earlier forecasts for a cold end of winter and natural gas saber-rattling between Canada and the U.S. fueled the recent rally, he says. "I like to think we're going a little lower from here, as we're coming out of winter, heading into a shoulder, and there won't be that much demand in the coming weeks." The Nymex front month settles down 8.3% at $4.084/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: VeriQuant Asset Management Announces Strategic Partnership with CLS Capital Group, Secures $10 Million in First Funding Raise
data.description:
Grounded in Principles, Driven by Innovation.

DENVER, March 12, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — VeriQuant Asset Management, a leading financial services firm specializing in the small business finance asset class, is proud to announce its successful first capital raise of $10 million, facilitated through a new strategic partnership with CLS Capital Group LLC (CLS). This milestone marks a pivotal moment for VeriQuant as it continues to grow and innovate within the merchant cash advance (MCA) market.

VeriQuant, with over 30 years of expertise in quantitative investment strategies, provides retail and institutional investors with direct access to the MCA market, a traditionally fragmented sector. With a focus on diversification, risk management, and data-driven solutions, the firm is committed to delivering risk-adjusted returns for its clients. By partnering with CLS Capital Group, a respected leader in financial services since 1993, VeriQuant is poised to unlock new investment opportunities and further enhance its growth trajectory.

“We are thrilled to announce this important milestone for VeriQuant,” said Nermin Catic, CEO of VeriQuant. “This partnership with CLS is a testament to the strength of our platform, our commitment to innovative, data-driven investment strategies, and our ability to attract top-tier investment partners. With this funding, we can continue to expand our platform and bring valuable opportunities to both retail and institutional investors.”

CLS Capital Group, formed in 1993 and known for its robust network of venture capital, private equity firms, and top financial institutions, brings a wealth of experience in providing funding solutions to a wide range of financial sectors. The alliance with VeriQuant strengthens CLS’s position in the growing small business finance space, offering a broader array of investment opportunities to its extensive investor network.

“This partnership with VeriQuant represents a dynamic shift in how we approach investment opportunities in the small business finance sector,” said Ray Uballe, President of CLS Capital Group. “VeriQuant’s advanced AI-powered platform and rigorous due diligence process will drive transparent, risk-conscious investments that are aligned with the interests of our investors. We look forward to collaborating closely with the team as they continue to redefine asset management in this evolving market.”

VeriQuant’s cutting-edge investment platform leverages AI and big data to evaluate over 45,000 loan applications annually, ensuring that all investments adhere to strict credit criteria. With this new funding, VeriQuant aims to scale its operations, optimize its proprietary platform, and continue delivering high-return investment opportunities with strong risk mitigation strategies.

About VeriQuant Asset Management

VeriQuant Asset Management is a global financial services firm focused on offering innovative, data-driven investment solutions in the small business finance sector. Specializing in merchant cash advances (MCA), VeriQuant offers investors access to high-quality, risk-adjusted opportunities while utilizing a proprietary platform for rigorous credit assessments and due diligence. VeriQuant is committed to empowering retail and institutional investors with secure, high-return opportunities in a historically fragmented market.

For more information, please visit: www.veriquant.net

Contact:

Nermin Catic, CEO

Email: nermin@veriquant.net

Mack McKaughan, COO

Email: mack@veriquant.net

About CLS Capital Group LLC

CLS Capital Group LLC (CLS) is a financial services firm that has been providing funding solutions to a broad spectrum of industries since 1993. Originally established as a mortgage lender and broker, CLS has expanded its reach to include a wide range of financial sectors, including venture capital, private equity, and investment banking. The firm maintains strategic alliances with top financial institutions, investment banks, and accredited investors, offering solutions to most funding requests.

For more information, please visit: www.clslending.net

VeriQuant Asset Management – Grounded in Principles, Driven by Innovation.

data.title: SATS ASA: SATS’ share buyback program has been completed
data.description:
Reference is made to the stock exchange announcement made by SATS ASA ("SATS" or the "Company") on 11 February 2025 of the launch of SATS’ program to repurchase up to 10,000,000 of the Company’s shares in open market transactions for a total amount of up to NOK 100 million during the period from 17 February 2025, until no later than 25 April 2025, the last trading day before to the Company’s 2025 Annual General Meeting, pursuant to an agreement with DNB Markets.

SATS has now completed the share buyback program. A total of 3,222,237 shares, which equals 1.69% of the shares in the company, has been purchased on Oslo Stock Exchange, for a total consideration of NOK 99,988,713.90. The average price paid per share was NOK 31.0308. Following this, SATS owns a total of 3,408,778 own shares.

Stine Klund, Investor Relations, phone: +47 986 99 259

This announcement contains information that is subject to disclosure requirements pursuant to the Market Abuse Regulation and section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

data.title: Lowey Dannenberg Notifies Novo Nordisk A/S (“Novo” or the “Company”) (NYSE: NVO) Investors of Securities Class Action Lawsuit and Encourages Investors with more than $200,000 in Losses to Contact the Firm
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 12, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Lowey Dannenberg P.C., a preeminent law firm in obtaining redress for consumers and investors, announces the filing of a class action lawsuit against Novo Nordisk A/S (“Novo” or the “Company”) for violations of the federal securities laws on behalf of investors who purchased or acquired Novo common stock between November 2, 2022 to December 19, 2024, inclusive (the “Class Period”).

On January 24, 2025, a complaint was filed against the Company and certain of its officers, alleging that throughout the Class Period, Defendants made false and/or misleading statements and/or failed to disclose that: (i) defendants created the false impression that they possessed reliable information pertaining to Novo’s projected successful outcome of Novo’s phase 3 CagriSema study on obesity, named “REDEFINE-1,” while avoiding discussions centered around dosage tolerability; (ii) Novo’s repeated optimistic claims that CagriSema would achieve at least 25% weight loss in the REDEFINE-1 study fell short of reality; and (iii) the utilization of the “flexible protocol” limited the study’s ability to effectively provide weight loss data on the dosage tested, suggesting either that tolerability was significantly worse than anticipated, resulting in patients titrating down their dosages to avoid complications, or that the patient selection process was rushed, leading to the onboarding of patients that did not desire to even achieve the 25% weight loss Novo sought to demonstrate.

When investors learned the truth, Novo’s common stock declined precipitously, injuring investors.

If you suffered a loss of more than $200,000 in Novo’s securities, and wish to participate, or learn more, click here, or please contact our attorneys at (914) 733-7256 or via email to Andrea Farah (afarah@lowey.com) or Vincent R. Cappucci Jr. (vcappucci@lowey.com).

Any investor who wishes to serve as Lead Plaintiff must act before March 25, 2025.

About Lowey Dannenberg

Lowey Dannenberg is a national firm representing institutional and individual investors, who suffered financial losses resulting from corporate fraud and malfeasance in violation of federal securities and antitrust laws. The firm has significant experience in prosecuting multi-million-dollar lawsuits and has recovered billions of dollars on behalf of its clients.

Contact:

data.title: ICE Review: Canola Tumbles for Third Straight Day
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--The ICE Futures canola market resumed its downturn due to tariffs imposed by the United States and China.

An analyst said canola is testing its contract lows and it will need to "punch through them" in order to regain stability in the market.

Statistics Canada projected 2025-26 canola acres to total 21.646 million, down 1.7 percent from last year. The data was collected before the U.S. and China implemented tariffs on Canadian imports.

Chicago soyoil and European rapeseed were down while Malaysian palm oil is up. Crude oil was stronger due to a weaker U.S. dollar.

At mid-afternoon, the Canadian dollar was up nearly four-tenths of a U.S. cent compared to Tuesday's close. The Bank of Canada cut its key interest rate by 25 basis points at 2.75 percent earlier today.

There were 72,833 contracts traded on Wednesday, which compares with Tuesday when 84,708 contracts changed hands. Spreading accounted for 33,228 of the contracts traded. Settlement prices are in Canadian dollars per metric tonne.

data.title: Green Street Acquires Australian Property Journal to Expand Global Reach of Green Street News
data.description:
Green Street Acquires Australian Property Journal to Expand Global Reach of Green Street News

  • Global News Offering Now Covers More Than 20 Countries on 3 Continents --

NEWPORT BEACH, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 12, 2025--

Green Street, the leading provider of trusted commercial real estate intelligence and unbiased insights, has acquired Australian Property Journal — Australia's premier publication for commercial property and residential real estate covering REITs, investment sales, and leasing transactions — to expand its global Green Street News offering. The growth of Green Street News into Australia will enable Green Street to continue its mission to empower the global commercial real estate community.

"This expansion marks the latest major milestone in Green Street News' growth and Green Street's overall mission to empower the global Commercial Real Estate community. We are breaking ground not only in a new country, but on a new continent to provide the most actionable insights to our consumer base of high-level CRE professionals. We've assembled an incredible team of journalists with a fast-paced breaking news workflow to bring our readers not only the best market intelligence, but the biggest scoops that they'll be able to read here before anywhere else."

  • Jeff Stuek, Chief Executive Officer at Green Street

Australian Property Journal, published since 2003, covers the four core property sectors of office, industrial, retail and residential, as well as hotels and tourism, planning, legal and regulatory, rural and agribusiness, healthcare, aged care and retirement, and student accommodation. Comprehensive coverage is provided across all the major territories of Sydney, Melbourne, Perth, Adelaide, Brisbane, Hobart, and Canberra.

Nelson Yap, founder, owner, and lead journalist for Australian Property Journal, will join a new team of local journalists including Larry Schlesinger, Su-Lin Tan and Layton Holley. The team brings over 60 years of journalism experience with proven track records of breaking exclusives in the UK, South Africa, Hong Kong, Singapore, and across the Australian continent. Green Street will rebrand Australian Property Journal to Green Street News Australia which will bring global-level insights into the local investment strategies for subscribers.

Yap said: "After more than two decades of creating the leading online property publication in Australia, I am elated that Australian Property Journal has been acquired by Green Street, the world's largest CRE publisher. As Australia's oldest digital property news publication and first online member of the Australian Press Council, Australian Property Journal has built a reputation of breaking property news.

"Green Street will grow the publication on a global scale, which is a vote of confidence for the Australian media industry and will create jobs in journalism. Our new team of journalists will deliver the latest breaking news to inform and empower the global CRE community."

Larry Schlesinger, Managing Editor for Green Street News Australia, also added: "I am delighted to be leading Green Street News' expansion into the Australian market. We have assembled a fantastic team of real estate journalists who have decades of combined experience, and I am confident we will become the leading source of exclusive commercial real estate news in Australia for our subscribers."

Green Street News Australia will bring global-level insights into the local investment strategies of subscribers. To learn more about the Green Street News Australia expansion visit greenstreetnews.com.

Australian Property was advised by Ted McDonnell, of McDonnell Advisory; along with David Leggatt and Andrew Du of Blue Rock Law. Green Street was advised by MinterEllison on the acquisition.

About Green Street

Green Street is the leading provider of actionable commercial real estate research, news, data, analytics, and advisory services in the U.S., Canada, and Europe. For nearly 40 years, Green Street has delivered unparalleled intelligence and trusted data on the public and private real estate markets, helping investors, banks, lenders, and other industry participants optimize investment and strategic decisions. The firm delivers exclusive market information, conclusion-driven insights, and predictive analytics through a SaaS platform. To learn more, please visit www.greenstreet.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250312753021/en/

CONTACT: Media Contact info:

Green Street

media@greenstreet.com

data.title: Egg Prices Starting to Fall, But Easter Looms — Market Talk
data.description:
1514 ET - The head of the American Egg Board said egg prices have started to come down as demand is moderating, but added Easter could see a second, temporary increase in prices. Emily Metz, the board's president and chief executive officer, said the disruption in egg supply is the result of highly pathogenic avian influenza, or bird flu. Metz added the a sustained period with no additional bird flu detections on egg farms will be needed before supplies can stabilize. Metz also said egg farmers are watching spring migration of wild birds, which are a leading cause of the spread of the bide flu virus. January saw a 91% increase of shell eggs from Mexico and Turkey, or more than 827,000 dozen, Metz said, which may help ease supply strain in the market. (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

1511 ET - Brown-Forman CEO Lawson Whiting believes that sales of spirits will rise once people have a bit more discretionary income. That is consumers are still buying booze--they are just currently trading down to smaller bottles that cost less, because their bank accounts have been squeezed by inflation, he says at the UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference. "They still want that affordable cocktail luxury at the end of the evening, but they're just switching down to a smaller size that's more affordable for them," Whiting says. "That's why we think a large part of this is cyclical and that it's going to turn." Shares fall 4.7%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1502 ET - The recent turbulence in financial markets is making corporate bonds look cheaper relative to Treasurys, but still not enough to compensate for the increased risk stemming from policy uncertainty tied to President Trump, Madison Investments' Mike Sanders says. The ICE BofA U.S. High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread has widened to 3.2% as of yesterday, from 2.6% in late January, according to FRED data. "We have been reducing some relative credit risk in our portfolios," Sanders says. He is buying more mortgages and owns "a good amount" of Treasurys. "We are just kind of watching the market and seeing if there is an opportunity to add either mortgages or credit." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1444 ET - Weight-loss drugs, weed and waning demand--particularly among Gen Z consumers--are what Brown-Forman CEO Lawson Whiting calls the big three dynamics hurting the U.S. spirits market. The rapid increase in the number of people that take GLP-1 agonists, or weight-loss drugs such as Ozempic and Wegovy, is meaningful, Whiting says: "We would be naive if we said that's not a long-term headwind." At the same time, cannabis use is on the rise, and younger consumers are drinking less. Whiting notes, though, that softer demand for spirits across Gen Z drinkers may be the result of economic inflation. That is because younger consumers are more often buying smaller portions of whiskey, which are less expensive, he says. Brown-Forman shares fall 5.3%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1438 ET - U.S. auto dealer views on electric vehicle sales improved in 1Q from the final quarter of 2024, but are still weak, Cox Automotive says in its latest Automotive Dealer Sentiment Index. The 1Q index indicates many dealers feel EV sales are worse now than a year ago, Cox says. Expectations for the EV market rose in 1Q, but the score of 40 shows most dealers expect the EV market will decline, rather than grow, over the next three months, according to Cox. The survey also shows dealers continue to view the EV tax credit favorably, citing its benefits for both dealerships and sales, Cox says. (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

1423 ET - The team at TD Securities says further rate cuts from the Bank of Canada will require "tangible signs of economic weakness" from the U.S.-Canada trade conflict. The firm predicts that scenario will unfold, based on some of the results from early polling the Bank of Canada conducted, showing trepidation among households and business leaders about spending plans. The BOC "is telegraphing a gradual approach to future policy decisions, but we do not see inflation expectations as a material hurdle to another quarter-point cut in April," TD says. "Our view is that the downward pressures on economic activity will ultimately dominate." (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

1421 ET - Front-month gold settles up 0.9% to $2,939.10 a troy ounce, making it the sixth session out of the past eight that front-month gold closed with a positive finish. The escalating tariff battle seen today — with the EU instituting retaliatory tariffs and Canada adding new levies — is helping propel gold, says Peter Cardillo of Spartan Capital Securities. "We believe gold is moving higher due to the prospects of the trade war intensifying in the coming weeks," he says. "The gold market is rising in response to potential inflation stemming from the trade war and uncertainties surrounding global economic activity."SPDR Gold rises 0.6%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1417 ET - Lawson Whiting, chief executive of Jack Daniel's maker Brown-Forman, says that the European Union's retaliatory tariffs on imports of American whiskey, among other goods, aren't a surprise. "There's been a lot of signals going on for a long time, particularly coming from the EU," he says at the UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference. "It's a tough spot," Whiting says, adding that it is good news the tariffs don't go into effect until April 1. "We've got a few weeks to be able to try to get these parties together and resolve the differences on this particular tariff conversation," he says. "It does give me some optimism that both the administration and the other counterparties over the last few weeks have been trying to resolve things relatively quickly." Brown-Forman off more than 5%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1412 ET - The U.S. imposed 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports earlier today, but President Trump had threatened a 50% tariff on Canadian steel and aluminum Tuesday, before relenting. That additional tariff would likely change trade flows, but not in a way that would benefit U.S. manufacturing, Alcoa CFO Molly Beerman says at the JPMorgan Industrials Conference. Under proposed higher tariffs, Alcoa would most likely send its aluminum produced in Canada to Europe, she says. "That supply may very well make its way over to the U.S.," Beerman says, adding that to have ships crossing the Atlantic with the same product just to get the right tariff treatment would be very disruptive. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1404 ET - BMO Capital Markets chief economist Doug Porter believes there are three more rate cuts coming from the Bank of Canada, regardless of the measured tone from Governor Tiff Macklem and the emphasis on tariff-related inflation risks. That would bring the BOC policy rate to 2%. Porter says BMO's call is based on the belief that Canada "will be facing some serious tariffs for an extended period of time and that the growth dampening aspects of the trade war will ultimately outweigh the upside inflationary impact." (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

1357 ET - Costco Wholesale's newest club opening in Massachusetts isn't closely surrounded by competitors, supporting the case that the club segment has room to grow, says D.A. Davidson's Michael Baker in a research note. Baker notes that the closest existing Costco by car is about 20 minutes from this new location, while the closest BJ's is a similar distance; Sam's Clubs, meanwhile, doesn't have a footprint in Massachusetts, with the closest one an hour away in New Hampshire. "This supports our view that the club segment has plenty of white space even with all three players in growth mode," says Baker. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1350 ET - The Public Company Accounting Oversight Board says it fined PricewaterhouseCoopers' Singapore unit $1.5 million for failing to have appropriate policies over testing to ensure that employees are free of conflicts of interest. Over nearly two years, employees in PwC Singapore's independence office understated the rates at which the firm failed to properly or timely report their financial interests and relationships, the regulator says. In some cases, staff misclassified certain failures as self-reported, the PCAOB says. The unit, which did not admit or deny the PCAOB's claims, agreed to work to improve how it monitors its independence processes. PwC Singapore said there was no breach of external independence rules and none of its clients were affected. "We are determined to learn from this and are committed to implementing measures to further strengthen compliance," the unit told WSJ. (mark.maurer@wsj.com; @markgmaurer)

data.title: EPA: Agency to Reconsider 'Clean Power Plan 2.0' Regulations on Power Plants

Lowey Dannenberg Notifies FTAI Aviation Ltd. (“FTAI” or the “Company”) (NASDAQ: FTAI) Investors of Securities Class Action Lawsuit and Encourages Investors with more than $100,000 in Losses to Contact the Firm
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 12, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Lowey Dannenberg P.C., a preeminent law firm in obtaining redress for consumers and investors, announces the filing of a class action lawsuit against FTAI Aviation Ltd. (“FTAI” or the “Company”) for violations of the federal securities laws on behalf of investors who purchased or acquired FTAI common stock between July 23, 2024 and January 15, 2025, inclusive (the “Class Period”).

On January 17, 2025, a complaint was filed against the Company and certain of its officers, alleging that throughout the Class Period, Defendants made false and/or misleading statements and/or failed to disclose that: (1) the Company reported one-time engine sales as Maintenance Repair & Overhaul revenue when FTAI only performs limited repair and maintenance work on the engine assets sold; (2) FTAI presents whole engine sales as individual module sales, thereby overstating sales and demand; (3) the Company depreciates engines that are not on lease, which misleadingly lowers the reported cost of goods sold and inflates EBITDA; and (4) that, as a result of the foregoing, Defendants’ positive statements about the Company’s business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis.

When investors learned the truth, FTAI’s common stock declined precipitously, injuring investors.

If you suffered a loss of more than $100,000 in FTAI’s securities, and wish to participate, or learn more, click here, or please contact our attorneys at (914) 733-7256 or via email to Andrea Farah (afarah@lowey.com) or Vincent R. Cappucci Jr. (vcappucci@lowey.com).

Any investor who wishes to serve as Lead Plaintiff must act before March 18, 2025.

About Lowey Dannenberg

Lowey Dannenberg is a national firm representing institutional and individual investors, who suffered financial losses resulting from corporate fraud and malfeasance in violation of federal securities and antitrust laws. The firm has significant experience in prosecuting multi-million-dollar lawsuits and has recovered billions of dollars on behalf of its clients.

Contact:

Lowey Dannenberg P.C.

44 South Broadway, Suite 1100

White Plains, NY 10601

data.title: Alphabet's Google Launches Latest Iteration of AI Model Gemma
data.description:
Alphabet's Google said Wednesday it has launched the latest version of its open source generative artificial intelligence model called Gemma 3.

The tech giant said Gemma 3 can be run on a single graphics processing unit or tensor processing unit. The latest version also introduces quantized models, which reduces model size and computational requirements, while maintaining high accuracy.

Google said it also launched ShieldGemma 2, an image safety checker built on the Gemma 3 foundation.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 3 PM ET
data.description:
Europe, Canada Hit Back at U.S. Steel Tariffs

The EU is targeting a range of American goods, including whiskey and motorcycles, with 50% tariffs, taking aim at politically sensitive products.

Inflation Cooled to 2.8% in February, Lower Than Expected

Inflation eased last month, but the data may offer less comfort to businesses, consumers and the Fed than it otherwise would as looming tariffs may keep prices rising.

Stocks Fluctuate on Muted Inflation Data, Loud Trade Fight

U.S. stocks were mostly higher in volatile trading as investors weighed the latest trade skirmish with Canada and a solid inflation report.

Trump set to nominate Michelle Bowman as Fed's top bank supervisor

President Donald Trump is set to nominate Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman to be the central bank's top bank supervisor, a White House official confirmed to MarketWatch.

Bank of Canada Cuts Policy Rate to 2.75% as Economy Faces 'New Crisis'

The central bank must balance the risk of tariff-fueled higher inflation and the damage to growth from U.S. trade policy, Gov. Tiff Macklem says.

OPEC Sticks to Oil-Demand View Ahead of Output Hike

The cartel still expects demand to grow by 1.45 million barrels a day this year and 1.43 million barrels a day the next, bolstered by strong air travel demand and road mobility.

Wind and Solar Overtake Coal Power for First Time in U.S.

The two renewable energy sources accounted for 17% of the country's power mix while coal fell to a low of 15%. Natural gas remains by far the largest source of electricity, accounting for 43%.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise For Second Straight Week

U.S. crude oil inventories increased by 1.4 million barrels, with an increase in production and net imports, while gasoline stocks fell more than expected as demand picked up.

WTO's Trade Barometer Picks Up, Pointing to Resilient Flows

The World Trade Organization's trade barometer rose, but it warned increased purchases by those seeking to get ahead of threatened tariffs might exaggerate the health of the global trading system.

ECB Facing 'Exceptionally High' Levels of Uncertainty, Lagarde Says

A rise in European defense spending in response to doubts about the reliability of the U.S. as an ally could push inflation higher, but tariffs might have the opposite effect, the ECB president said.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Wednesday at 3 PM ET
data.description:
D-Wave Claims 'Quantum Supremacy,' Beating Traditional Computers

D-Wave is the latest quantum computing company to say it has achieved "quantum supremacy," better known as the ability to solve a problem with a quantum computer that can't be done with a traditional computer.

Puma Will Cut 500 Positions Globally as Part of a Cost-Cutting Plan

Job reductions will be implemented at the group's headquarters in Germany and in regional offices. The company has a total workforce of 22,500.

Walmart Gets an Earful From China Over Response to Trump Tariffs

Beijing authorities summoned Walmart officials for a dressing-down after receiving complaints that the retailer was pressuring some Chinese suppliers to cut prices.

Niantic to Sell Pokemon GO, Other Games to Saudi-Backed Group in $3.5 Billion Deal

Niantic is also selling "Pikmin Bloom," and "Monster Hunter Now," Niantic's most recent game.

Wonder Acquires Tastemade for Its Latest Quest: to Make a 'Mealtime Super App'

Wonder CEO Marc Lore hopes the company will one day offer AI-powered autonomous food ordering for all three meals of the day.

Rheinmetall Signals Booming Sales As Europe Enters 'Era Of Rearmament'

While 2024 sales grew 35%, boosted by increased demand, there was a dip in sales at its power systems and trade divisions at the start of of the year.

Whiskey Is a Target of Europe's Retaliatory Tariffs. Brown-Forman Stock Slides.

The European Union plans to establish countermeasures in April that apply to products ranging from boats to bourbon.

Target Beefs Up Grocery Supply Chain as Consumer Spending Shifts

The retailer is bringing its supply chain for fresh and frozen foods in house to better forecast demand and plan inventory.

Porsche Plans 3,900 Job Cuts in Efficiency Drive

The company warned investments and costs of reshaping will hit earnings this year and expects markets to remain challenging.

Roomba Maker iRobot Warns of Going Concern, Initiates Strategic Review

iRobot warned investors of a going concern to maintain enough assets to operate and pay its debts, and said it has initiated a strategic review of the business.

data.title: UAE-Backed MGX Makes $2 Billion Investment In Crypto Exchange Binance — WSJ
data.description:
By Vicky Ge Huang

Binance said on Wednesday that United Arab Emirates state-backed investor MGX has acquired a minority stake in the world's largest crypto exchange for $2 billion.

The transaction was paid for in stablecoin, a popular type of cryptocurrency that is often pegged to government-issued currencies like the U.S dollar. It marks the first institutional investment in Binance, the company said. It also represents MGX's first foray into the crypto and blockchain sector, according to an MGX-Binance press release.

The deal is the latest example of a reversal of fortunes for crypto companies like Binance under the Trump administration. Last month, the Securities and Exchange Commission asked a court to pause its case against Binance. In 2023, the SEC, then led by Gary Gensler, charged Binance and founder Changpeng Zhao with a slew of securities violations.

That year, Binance also pleaded guilty to violating U.S. anti-money-laundering requirements and agreed to pay a $4.3 billion fine. Zhao served four months in jail on a related charge.

Binance said it employs about 1,000 people out of its 5,000-person global workforce in the UAE. The exchange said it has more than 260 million registered users.

MGX, an artificial intelligence fund led by Sheikh Tahnoon bin Zayed Al Nahyan, was one of the few names mentioned backing Stargate, a $500 billion data center project announced in January at the White House to be led by SoftBank and OpenAI.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: EU ALERT: Levi & Korsinsky Reminds Investors of an Investigation Involving Possible Securities Fraud Violations by enCore Energy Corp.
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / March 12, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of enCore Energy Corp. ("enCore Energy Corp.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

enCore reported financial results for the quarter ended December 31, 2024, announcing that Mr. Goranson was no longer serving as CEO or as a member of the Board. enCore also revealed that, "our management identified material weaknesses in our internal control over financial reporting as of December 31, 2024."

Following this news, the price of the Company's stock dropped on March 3, 2025. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/encore-energy-corp-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=135579&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Gerdau Initiated at Neutral by UBS
City Developments' billionaire executive chair drops legal case against CEO son
data.description:
Singapore's City Developments on Wednesday said its billionaire executive chairman, Kwek Leng Beng, had dropped a lawsuit against his son, the company's CEO, whom he had accused of plotting a boardroom coup.

The firm was plunged into a power struggle late last month and had suspended trading in its shares after Leng Beng said he had filed court papers to remove his son, Sherman Kwek, from his position.

City Developments said Leng Beng will continue to serve as executive chairman while Kwek will remain as the group's chief executive officer. All current directors will remain on the board.

"All the board members have agreed to put aside their differences for the greater good of CDL and its stakeholders," Leng Beng said in a statement late Wednesday.

City Developments ended trading at S$5.12 on February 25 before going on a trading halt a day later.

The stock closed Wednesday at S$4.94, 3.5% lower than before the feud was first publicly revealed.

This latest development seems to be an important step toward mending a deep family divide that became public in February.

data.title: US natural gas prices fall more than 8% on record output, milder weather outlook
data.description:
Key points:
  • Record production levels contribute to price drop
  • Milder weather predicted for next two weeks
  • Canadian gas exports to US decline post-tariffs

U.S. natural gas futures fell more than 8% on Wednesday, driven by record production levels and predictions of milder weather over the next two weeks than previously expected, which could dampen heating demand and enable utilities to draw less gas from storage.

Front-month gas futures for April delivery on the New York Mercantile Exchange settled 36.9 cents, or 8.3% lower at $4.08 per million British thermal units (mmBtu). Prices rose to their highest level since December 2022 on Monday but fell 0.9% in the previous session.

"I think the run-up this week was largely inspired by technical factors and bigger macro funds, but the weather this week has been rather bearish, so I think today's action is just really a reflection," said Kyle Cooper, energy market analyst at IAF Advisors.

Financial firm LSEG forecast average gas demand in the Lower 48 states, including exports, will fall from 111.6 bcfd this week to 104.3 bcfd next week. This was a decrease from Tuesday's forecast of 113.3 bcfd for next week.

Meanwhile, LSEG said average gas output in the Lower 48 U.S. states has risen to 105.8 billion cubic feet per day (bcfd) so far in March, up from a record 105.1 bcfd in February.

LSEG estimated there would be 198 heating degree days over the next two weeks in the Lower 48 U.S. states, down from the 213 HDDs estimated on Tuesday. The normal level is 243 HDDs for this time of year.

Gas prices rose more than 14% last week on record flows to LNG plants and worries Canada would reduce power and gas exports to the U.S. after President Donald Trump imposed tariffs on Canada and Mexico on March 4. Trump later said the two trading partners would not have to pay tariffs until early April on any goods that fell under the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement.

The premier of the Canadian province of Ontario said on Tuesday he was suspending plans to impose a 25% surcharge on electricity exports to the U.S. and would fly to Washington this week for talks with the Trump administration.

Canada in 2024 supplied about 8% of total U.S. gas demand, including exports, and about 1% of total U.S. power demand, again including exports. Some of those power and gas exports returned to Canada.

Canadian gas exports to the U.S. have dropped to an average of 8.7 bcfd since Trump's tariffs were announced, down from an average of 9.8 bcfd during the prior 11-day period from February 21 to March 3, according to LSEG data.

That compares with an average of 8.6 bcfd of Canadian gas exports to the U.S. in 2024 and 7.6 bcfd over the prior five years (2019-2023).

The amount of gas flowing to the eight big U.S. LNG export plants has risen to an average of 15.7 bcfd so far in March, up from a record 15.6 bcfd in February, as new units at Venture Global's 3.2-bcfd Plaquemines LNG export plant under construction in Louisiana enter service.

Meanwhile, Dutch and British wholesale gas prices were mixed, with colder weather spurring demand, and as the market awaits further clarity over a potential ceasefire in the Ukraine war after a recent step-up of attacks.

data.title: Investar Holding Corp SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Investar Holding Corp, a financial services company operating primarily in south Louisiana, southeast Texas, and Alabama, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Investar Holding Corp reported the following key financial metrics for 2024:

  • Net Interest Income: $69.8 million, a decrease of 6.4% from $74.5 million in 2023, primarily due to higher rates paid on interest-bearing liabilities.
  • Net Income: $20.3 million, an increase of 21.4% from $16.7 million in 2023, driven by a $7.7 million increase in noninterest income.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $2.04, up from $1.69 in 2023, reflecting the increase in net income.
  • Return on Average Assets: 0.73%, up from 0.60% in 2023, indicating improved asset utilization.
  • Return on Average Equity: 8.60%, up from 7.63% in 2023, driven by higher net income.

Business Highlights

Investar Holding Corp's business performance in 2024 included the following highlights:

  • Geographical Performance: The company operates 29 full-service branches, with a significant presence in Baton Rouge, New Orleans, Lafayette, Lake Charles, and their surrounding areas. Approximately 78% of total deposits were from south Louisiana as of December 31, 2024.
  • Lending Activities: The company focuses on commercial real estate loans, which accounted for approximately 49% of total loans. Commercial and industrial loans made up about 25%, while construction and development loans accounted for 7% of total loans.
  • Lending to Individuals: Residential real estate loans, including second mortgages, comprised approximately 19% of total loans. Consumer loans represented less than 1% of total loans.
  • Branch Network Efficiency: Investar has been optimizing its branch network by closing four branches and selling three tracts of land held for future branch locations over the last three fiscal years. This strategy aims to leverage digital initiatives and reduce costs.
  • Exit from Consumer Mortgage Origination: In the third quarter of 2023, the company exited the consumer mortgage origination business to focus on more profitable products and services.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue focusing on consistent, quality earnings through balance sheet optimization and considers acquisitions on an opportunistic basis. Their long-term strategy includes organic growth through high-quality loans and growth through acquisitions.
  • Digital Banking Initiatives: Investar has been enhancing its digital banking capabilities, including converting a loan and deposit production office in Alabama to a cashless branch designed to provide a digital banking experience.
  • Regulatory Environment: The company is subject to extensive regulation, including the Dodd-Frank Act, which impacts its operations and compliance costs. Changes in regulations could materially affect the company's financial condition and operations.
  • Human Capital Resources: As of December 31, 2024, Investar employed 327 full-time and eight part-time employees. The company emphasizes attracting and retaining exceptional employees and offers a comprehensive benefits package.

Strategic Initiatives

Investar Holding Corp's strategic initiatives and capital management activities in 2024 included:

  • Strategic Shift: In 2023, the company pivoted its strategy from growth to focusing on consistent, quality earnings through balance sheet optimization. This included originating high-quality, primarily variable-rate loans and allowing higher-risk credit relationships to run off. The company also exited the consumer mortgage origination business to focus on shorter-duration, higher risk-adjusted return asset classes.
  • Acquisitions: The company continued to evaluate acquisition opportunities and completed the purchase of commercial and industrial revolving lines of credit totaling $162.7 million.
  • Capital Management: The company repurchased $8.0 million in principal amount of subordinated debt and redeemed $20.0 million in principal amount of its 2029 Notes. It also repurchased 18,621 shares of common stock at an average price of $16.13 per share in 2024. Dividends were increased by 4% to $0.41 per share for 2024. The company utilized the Federal Reserve's Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) for liquidity, which was fully repaid by the end of 2024. Stockholders’ equity increased by 6.4% to $241.3 million at the end of 2024.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its strategy of optimizing the balance sheet and focusing on high-quality loan origination. It will also explore further acquisition opportunities and maintain a focus on enhancing shareholder value through strategic capital management activities, including potential share repurchases and dividend increases. The company anticipates continued pressure on net interest margin if the yield on interest-earning assets decreases faster than the cost of funds.

Challenges and Risks

Investar Holding Corp faces several challenges and risks, including:

  • Economic and Geopolitical Risks: The company's operations are sensitive to economic conditions and geopolitical events, such as inflation, interest rate changes, and political tensions, which can impact loan demand and credit quality.
  • Interest Rate Risks: Fluctuations in interest rates can affect the company's net interest income and the valuation of its assets and liabilities. High interest rates have increased the cost of deposits, pressuring net interest margins.
  • Liquidity Risks: The company relies on customer deposits and other funding sources, which could be affected by market conditions and customer perceptions of safety, especially following high-profile bank failures.
  • Credit Risks: The adoption of the CECL accounting standard has increased the variability of credit loss estimates, potentially impacting earnings. The concentration in commercial real estate and industrial loans poses additional risks.
  • Operational Risks: The company faces challenges from technological changes and cybersecurity threats, which could disrupt operations and damage reputation.
  • Regulatory Risks: The financial services industry is highly regulated, and changes in laws or increased scrutiny could limit growth and profitability.
  • Environmental Risks: Climate change and related legislative responses could impact the company's operations and those of its customers.

Management has pivoted its strategy to focus on consistent, quality earnings through balance sheet optimization. This includes exiting the consumer mortgage origination business and enhancing digital banking capabilities. However, the success of this strategy is uncertain, and the company must effectively manage risks associated with interest rates, credit quality, and competition.

SEC Filing: Investar Holding Corp [ ISTR ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2025

data.title: AMES NATIONAL CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Ames National Corporation, a financial holding company operating primarily in Iowa, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. Ames National Corporation operates six banks across central, north-central, and south-central Iowa, focusing on agricultural, commercial, and residential real estate lending.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Interest Income: $44.976 million, representing a 0.8% increase from 2023, primarily driven by changes in interest rates and loan volume.
  • Net Income: $10.218 million, a decrease of 5.5% compared to 2023, primarily due to increased salaries, benefits, and one-time consultant fees.
  • Basic and Diluted Earnings Per Share: $1.14, down from $1.20 in 2023, reflecting the decrease in net income.
  • Return on Assets: 0.48%, compared to 0.51% in 2023, indicating a slight decrease in asset efficiency.
  • Return on Equity: 6.02%, down from 7.05% in 2023, reflecting reduced profitability.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The principal sources of revenue for Ames National Corporation include interest and fees on loans, interest on investments, fees on wealth management services, service charges on deposit accounts, merchant and card fees, gains on the sale of loans, and securities gains. The largest component contributing to net income is net interest income.
  • Geographical Performance: All operations are conducted in Iowa, primarily within the central, north-central, and south-central counties. The company operates six banks, each serving specific communities such as Ames, Nevada, Boone, Story City, Marshalltown, and Creston. Each bank focuses on local businesses and residents, with a strong emphasis on agricultural, commercial, and residential real estate lending.
  • Sales Units: The banks' lending activities include commercial, multi-family, and agricultural real estate loans, residential real estate loans, agricultural and business operating loans, equipment loans, vehicle loans, personal loans, and home improvement loans. The banks also originate mortgage loans for sale into the secondary market.
  • New Product Launches: The banks provide innovative financial services such as online banking, mobile banking, private banking, and wealth management to meet evolving customer needs.
  • Future Outlook: Management has identified potential challenges such as elevated short-term interest rates, commodity price fluctuations, and economic conditions affecting the agricultural sector. The company is focused on managing interest rate risk and maintaining a strong return on equity by leveraging local decision-making and seasoned management to provide competitive products and services.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Liquidity Management: The company is focused on maintaining a strong liquidity position by managing its balance sheet effectively. This includes a strategic reduction in the investment portfolio by $87.9 million, primarily due to maturities exceeding purchases, and an increase in net loans by 2.0% to $1.30 billion.
  • Capital Management: The company increased its total stockholders' equity to $174.7 million as of December 31, 2024, from $165.8 million in 2023, primarily due to a decrease in unrealized losses on the investment portfolio and retention of net income in excess of dividends. The company repurchased 43,057 shares of common stock under stock repurchase plans in 2024. Dividends from the Banks to the Company amounted to $10.2 million in 2024, supporting the company's liquidity needs. The company maintains a strong capital position, exceeding regulatory guidelines to be considered 'well capitalized.'
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its focus on liquidity management and capital adequacy, ensuring sufficient resources to meet financial commitments and support growth. The company anticipates maintaining its capital levels and liquidity position, with the potential to adjust dividend payments to strengthen its financial standing. The company will also continue to monitor interest rate environments and economic conditions to manage risks effectively.

Challenges and Risks

  • Economic and Market Condition Risks: The company faces risks from changes in general business, economic, and political conditions, including inflationary pressures that have persisted through 2024. The Federal Open Market Committee's interest rate increases to control inflation could overly dampen economic activity, potentially leading to a recession, which would adversely affect the company's financial condition.
  • Credit Risks: The company must manage credit risk effectively, particularly in its commercial real estate loan portfolio, which is subject to fluctuating collateral values. Loans to agricultural-related borrowers are also at risk due to factors beyond the company's control, such as commodity price fluctuations and government trade policies.
  • Liquidity and Interest Rate Risks: Changes in interest rates could adversely affect the company's results, as its interest-earning assets and interest-bearing liabilities do not react uniformly to rate changes. The company also faces risks related to maintaining adequate liquidity, which is essential for its operations.
  • Operational Risks: The company is exposed to risks such as data processing system failures, data security breaches, and customer or employee fraud. Cybersecurity threats have increased with greater reliance on remote working, posing significant risks to the company's operations and reputation.
  • Strategic and External Risks: The company may face challenges in continuing to grow, and its growth may strain resources. It also faces competition from larger financial institutions and fintech companies, which could impact its market position.
  • Regulatory Risks: Current and future government regulations could increase the company's costs of doing business. The Dodd-Frank Act and other regulatory requirements impose significant compliance obligations.
  • Management Strategies: Management has identified inflation and interest rate changes as significant challenges. The company is focused on managing its interest rate spread and maintaining liquidity to mitigate these risks. It also emphasizes strong personal relationships and credit expertise to support its business strategy.
  • Market Risk Exposure: The company is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations, which could impact its net interest income. It monitors its interest rate risk exposure but acknowledges that it cannot fully protect against future interest rate risk exposure.

SEC Filing: AMES NATIONAL CORP [ ATLO ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2025

data.title: Aussie leisure travel stalled; Investors see further earnings downside
data.description:
** Analysts at Jefferies say data suggests leisure travel in Australia remains flat to slightly down for the agent sector in 2025

** Flags all three major travel agencies in Australia, namely Webjet , Web Travel and Flight Centre have been materially de-rated over the past year

** Brokerage says investors appear to be anticipating further earnings weakness as the macro-economic sentiment deteriorates

** Notes statement from Delta Air Lines saying that March quarter outlook is impacted by macro uncertainty

** WJL collectively down over 6% in the last three sessions, while WEB has lost about 10% in the last four days

** FLT down 8% in the past three sessions collectively
